hotel_name: Khách sạn Hướng Dương Thanh Hóa (Huong Duong Hotel Thanh Hoa)

reviews.title: The hotel but the quality is not as good as a guesthouse.
reviews.text:
- Booked a hotel room but when checking in it turned out to be a guesthouse, the room smelled bad.
- The staff, when transferring the exact amount with cents, asked, "Did you manage to transfer the cents too?" What does that mean? If the price is X, then transfer exactly X. Should I round it up to transfer even amounts? The reception staff thinking like this drives customers away, discriminates against customers, and shows a lack of respect for them.
- When checking in, the receptionist said, "Uncle security, please take the guests to the guesthouse area," which felt disheartening. Thinking back, why is this hotel treating customers with such discrimination? Both the rich and the poor have their self-esteem. If they only value the rich and treat the poor with a service attitude like this, then the hotel owner's mindset is clearly not developed enough to operate in this service field, or he simply doesn’t care to intervene and lets staff take care of customers in their own way.
- And finally, I hope the owner changes their approach so that customers feel happier when staying here, for a longer lasting experience and to bring more value.

reviews.title: Terrible Experience: No Flexibility and Poor Room Conditions
reviews.text: I booked a standard room. Upon check-in, I didn’t like the room type, so I called the front desk to request a paid upgrade to a Superior room. However, the receptionist responded: “No, if you want to change to a different room type, you must forfeit 100% of the amount you already paid and make a new booking, because Agoda guests are treated differently from guests who book directly with the hotel.” This was extremely unreasonable. The hotel showed no flexibility even though I was willing to pay extra for the upgrade. I immediately left and accepted losing the money. I couldn’t stay in a place that made me so upset. The room was small, the bed creaked, and the bathroom was dirty.

reviews.title: I don't recommend this place for a family
reviews.text: First time is here, the last time also

reviews.title: Room smaller than picture
reviews.text: They checked me in and gave me a smaller room that what I booked. Why?

reviews.title: Criticism.
reviews.text: The positives are the enthusiastic dining room staff and that the hotel is new, so the amenities are decent. However, the room does not look like the pictures, there is no vanity table, the electrical outlets are loose, and there is no hot water.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.text: Ok

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text: I booked through the app, but the links provided did not clearly specify the differences between the guesthouse and the hotel area. This would help guests choose the right type of room when booking. The staff was not friendly. When I arrived to check in, the staff responded with a bit of an attitude, which made for a poor experience. However, the room was quite clean.

reviews.title: A beautiful hotel but not very stable yet.
reviews.text: The hotel is beautiful and clean as it has only been operating for a few months, but it's not very professional yet. The staff is friendly but quite lacking, so the receptionists end up doing room checks, which led to me forgetting my belongings and the staff not noticing. When I returned to Hanoi and called to ask them to check, they also couldn't respond immediately. The breakfast buffet is quite poor, especially lacking in vegetables.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: Ok for 1 night trip.

reviews.title: So close...
reviews.text:
So close to getting a great review. The hotel itself is pretty nice. The room was perfect for my needs. Comfortable bed, desk as a workspace, pretty good lighting. Coffee/tea/kettle. (Though, as with most hotels, nobody thinks to put an electrical outlet anywhere near the coffee service)

The reason it missed a great review was because of lack of proper maintenance. I could see marble cracked and broken around the doorframes, and the double doors on my floor near the elevator were not operating properly because of this. My main issue was that I wanted a hot shower and didn't get it. I asked at the desk, they radioed someone, and then curtly nodded at me to go away. I know there was a language barrier, but it was a little disconcerting. 

I went back upstairs and ran the tap in the sink. Yes, the water was now hot. But I'd already taken a cold shower. Later, after I had gone out for dinner, I came back and had another cold shower.

The next morning before I showered, I again went downstairs to ask them to do something about it. Same thing. Radio someone. Give me a sullen look. Wave me away. 

Shower was hot. For exactly one minute. Then it went cold again. 

So, evidently there is an electric heater unit buried in a wall somewhere and it keeps tripping out. I guarantee the problem didn't begin with my stay. Management needs to fix their shit. 

One bright spot was the security guard. I believe his name was Huong, but I'm not sure. He didn't speak any English, rather, he knew German. But he was so kind and helpful. I really appreciated him. 

I would still recommend the hotel. It was a nice place, new, beautiful. But like most new, beautiful, comfortable hotels in southeast Asia, it won't stay that way unless management puts some time and effort (money) into proper maintenance of their facilities.

reviews.title: The best value hotel.
reviews.text: It's a cost-effective hotel in Tanghwa. The air conditioner is an LG dual inverter and there's also a fan, so it was very cool and nice. It's hard to find such a hotel at this price. For one person, the standard room is okay. I highly recommend the superior room for couples!

reviews.title: Worth trying.
reviews.text: The room is clean, tidy, and the service is good. Although I encountered a minor issue with my room card, the reception and security resolved it quite quickly for me.

reviews.title: Fairly okay.
reviews.text: Nice location, the room is quite small and stuffy, and since the hotel was empty, I was arranged to stay in the inner building instead of the outer one; the inner building is quite old.
reviews.response:
Dạ Hướng Dương Hotel xin cảm ơn quý khách đã trải nghiệm dịch vụ. Quý khách đang đặt phòng là phòng đơn thuộc khu toà bên trong là Motel  . Khu toà bên ngoài là Hotel ạ. Mỗi giá hạng phòng dịch vụ khác nhau ạ.
Hy vọng vào dịp gần nhất quý khách sẽ có trải nghiệm tại Hướng Dương Hotel ạ.
Trân trọng cảm ơn quý khách!

reviews.title: Wonderful
reviews.text: The hotel is extremely clean, and the arrangement of furniture is quite elegant. Although it is a luxurious room, the size is smaller than expected. However, cleanliness is the most important thing, so it gets a 10/10. You can see that every grout line in the bathroom is pristine white, and the faucet is sparkling.

reviews.text: The hotel is far from the center. The area where I stay is an old neighborhood. The interior has deteriorated. It's a cheap place for temporary accommodation.

reviews.title: Room not as advertised

reviews.title: Sunflower Hotel, Thanh Hoa
reviews.text:
My family: 2 adults and 3 children, including one older child (over 18 years old) and 2 younger children (12 years old), has stayed at the Huong Duong Hotel twice during our trip to central Vietnam.

My impression of the hotel is positive, suitable for families or individual guests staying for work or tourism: The rooms are clean and newly equipped; The lobby area is spacious, clean, and beautifully decorated; The breakfast is delicious with a variety of options...

The hotel also has parking space for guests traveling by personal car.

If I have the chance to travel to Thanh Hoa again, I will choose to stay at the Huong Duong Hotel once more.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text: The room is not large but very clean, well-equipped. The staffs are not all friendly but ok. The guard is caring. I got good deal on Agoda, so it's exceptional for the price.

reviews.title: Good quality
reviews.text: The staff is very enthusiastic, especially the security guards. The hotel is right on the main road, making it convenient to get around, and there are many eateries nearby.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text: There are very enthusiastic security guards. I booked through Agoda, but the staff were not very knowledgeable, so it took a bit longer. The location and amenities are fine, and in the evening near the hotel there is a restaurant serving delicious rice rolls and eel vermicelli, and there is a nearby Winmart shop convenient for buying supplies.

reviews.title: Wonderful
reviews.text: The hotel is convenient, clean, centrally located and easy to find, with prices that are affordable compared to the quality. The reception staff is friendly and enthusiastic.

reviews.title: Clean hotel
reviews.text: My family drove from Hue to Quang Ninh and stopped for one night at Thanh Hoa, choosing Hướng Dương Hotel. We arrived at the hotel around 11 p.m., and there was a bit of confusion, but the receptionist quickly helped us handle it so we could go to our room and rest. Compared to private hotels, Hướng Dương is a clean hotel, relatively nicely designed, with polite staff and very good prices that everyone should consider if they have the opportunity to visit Thanh Hoa. Thank you, Hướng Dương.

reviews.title: travel

reviews.title: Not 4 star - imo.
reviews.text: Walls where not entirely clean, bed was pretty hard to lay in, and there was people smoking in the halls and in their rooms. Inevitably reaching into yours.

reviews.title: Evaluation of Huong Duong Hotel (Motel)
reviews.text:
First experience staying in Huong Duong Hotel (Motel) for a 2-nights booking, is also be the last there.

Upon after checking-in the property, found there are misleading information as described in Agoda booking site, for example welcome drinks are not served as included and hotel lobby shown in Agoda is a total contrast in actual where the corridor to the room is dimly lighted and gloomy.

One the second day of stay, as we came back to the “hotel” room in the evening hours, found the room wasn’t cleaned and fresh towels were not provided, with all the used towels on the first day still remaining in the room. When approached the hotel staff, we were told any room cleaning service needed has to be notified.

Last but not least, we found a House Rule posted on one of it’s wall and mentioned that is Huong Duong Motel. This property is more to serves hourly guests or 1-night stand couples, surely not suitable for families or decent guests.

reviews.title: Nice hotel with friendly staff
reviews.text: Breakfast was great with continental and Asian cuisine.

reviews.title: Great hotel
reviews.text: All you need for a great nights sleep anddrest
